title: Add policy validation Job before Deployment rollout
summary: The policy validation sub-reconciler is well-implemented, follows all established patterns (job.RunJob lifecycle, sub-reconciler condition contract, restrictedSecurityContext, delete-on-nil-spec), and has comprehensive test coverage (17 unit tests + 2 integration tests all passing). One critical finding: the new +kubebuilder:rbac pods marker is not synced to the Helm chart RBAC template, which will cause 403 errors for getValidationErrorMessage when deployed via Helm.

tests_checklist.item: Integration tests exist and pass (2 tests, //go:build integration tagged)
Job builder spec verified (image, security, command, backoff, TTL, mounts)
ObservedGeneration verified on all 4 condition paths
Integration: gating behavior (Deployment blocked until validation passes)
Integration: NotRequired path (no policy, no Job)
SecurityContext verified via shared expectRestrictedSecurityContext helper

code_quality_checklist.item: Uses shared job.RunJob library for Job lifecycle
Uses shared conditions.SetCondition for condition management
Delete-on-nil pattern matches deleteCronJob and deleteNetworkPolicy
Job name derived from CR name (multi-instance safe)
No dead code or unnecessary abstractions

security_checklist.item: No sensitive data in condition messages
Termination message truncated to 500 chars (prevents oversized status)
RBAC marker synced with Helm chart

architecture_checklist.item: PolicyValidReady positioned correctly in subConditionTypes (before DeploymentReady)
Reconciliation call order correct (after NetworkPolicy, before Deployment)
Early-exit pattern gates Deployment on PolicyValidReady
Stale Job detection via pod-spec hash (job.RunJob built-in)
Solution is minimal — no unnecessary abstractions

dry_yagni_checklist.item: No duplicated code (reuses job.RunJob, restrictedSecurityContext, conditions.SetCondition)
No speculative features or unnecessary configurability
Delete helper follows established inline pattern (not over-abstracted)

fail_fast_checklist.item: Descriptive error messages extracted from Pod termination messages
Fallback message with kubectl command when termination message unavailable

defensive_checklist.item: Missing Pod for error extraction handled (fallback message)
Message truncation prevents oversized condition messages

security_findings.description: New +kubebuilder:rbac marker for pods (get, list) added to keystone_controller.go but not synced to the Helm chart RBAC template in _helpers.tpl. When deployed via Helm, the operator will receive 403 Forbidden errors when getValidationErrorMessage() attempts to list pods for error extraction. The feature degrades gracefully (falls back to generic error message) but provides a worse user experience.
security_findings.fix: Add a new RBAC rule block to the rbacRules template in _helpers.tpl: apiGroups: [""], resources: [pods], verbs: [get, list] with a CC-0058 comment. Also update role.yaml sync comment if applicable.

issues_found.description: RBAC pods rule missing from Helm chart. The controller adds +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=core,resources=pods,verbs=get;list but the Helm _helpers.tpl rbacRules template has no corresponding rule. The core group rules only cover services/configmaps/secrets/serviceaccounts and events. This means Helm-deployed operators cannot list pods for error message extraction from failed validation Jobs.
issues_found.fix:
Add between the events rule (line 132) and the batch rule (line 133): # core - pods (CC-0058) 
- apiGroups:
    - ""
  resources:
    - pods
  verbs:
    - get
    - list
issues_found.evidence: keystone_controller.go:62 has `// +kubebuilder:rbac:groups=core,resources=pods,verbs=get;list` but grep for 'pods' in the Helm templates directory returns zero matches. The _helpers.tpl core rules at lines 110-132 list only: services, configmaps, secrets, serviceaccounts (lines 113-116) and events (lines 128-129).

suggested_improvements: Consider adding a Chainsaw E2E test under tests/e2e/keystone/ for the policy validation lifecycle to complement the envtest integration tests, especially for testing with a real oslopolicy-checker binary.
next_steps: Add pods get/list RBAC rule to Helm chart _helpers.tpl and verify role.yaml sync comment
Run Helm chart unit tests to verify the new RBAC rule renders correctly
